{{Infobox book
| name = Unsouled
| slug = cradle-01-unsouled
| precedes = [[Soulsmith (novel)|Soulsmith]]
| follows = [[Of Killers and Kings (novel)|Of Killers and Kings]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-01-unsouled.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2017
| isbn = 9781734958171
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 292
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|In the [[Sacred Valley]], seven-year-old [[Lindon]] is declared "[[Unsouled]]" after failing the [[Spiritual Origin test]], receiving only a wooden badge marked with the symbol for "empty." Despite this setback, [[Lindon]] shows resourcefulness by hunting a spirit-fruit and capturing its [[Remnant]]. Although his family debates how to divide the fruit, [[Lindon]] claims half, though it provides no immediate advancement. Seeking guidance from the ancient fox [[Elder Whisper]], [[Lindon]] receives cryptic encouragement to forge his own path. His resolve is tested when the [[Mon family]] challenges him to a duel; [[Lindon]] prepares by discovering the "[[Heart of Twin Stars]]" and practicing the "[[Empty Palm]]" technique, eventually using them to stagger a superior opponent and save his family's honor.

Seeking further advancement, [[Lindon]] negotiates with the [[First Elder]] to participate in the upcoming [[Seven-Year Festival]]. During this time, the [[Abidan]] [[Suriel]] arrives on the planet [[Cradle]] to investigate a spatial violation. As [[Lindon]] prepares for the festival by striking a deal with hornet Remnants, his mother, [[Seisha]], warns him of suspicious activities by the [[Li clan]] regarding spatial madra. During the festival, [[Lindon]] wins his matches using secret techniques, but the event turns tragic when [[Li Markuth]], a [[Gold-level]] [[Grand Patriarch]], arrives to settle a debt by murdering [[Lindon]]'s family. In the ensuing chaos, [[Lindon]] is killed while attempting to fight back.

However, [[Suriel]] intervenes by reversing causality, restoring [[Lindon]] to life and providing him with visions of his potential and a celestial token. Following this rebirth, [[Lindon]] uses a swarm of [[Remnants]] to defeat his cousin and earns a place as a disciple at the Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School. Upon arriving at [[Mount Samara]], he navigates the [[Trial of Glorious Ascension]] and uses deception to secure resources from the [[Lesser Treasure Hall]]. After being ambushed and beaten by a [[Kazan]] disciple named Ma Deret, [[Lindon]] recovers and seeks out [[Yerin]], the disciple of a fallen [[Sword Sage]]. The two form a soul-bound alliance to survive.

[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] navigate the dangers of the school, eventually killing Ma Deret and forming a plan to steal equipment from the [[Lesser Treasure Hall]]. Their heist leads them into conflict with [[Elder Whitehall]], a greedy man seeking the [[Sword Sage]]'s treasures. After escaping the school via a stolen [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]], they arrive at the Ancestor's Tomb{{!}}Ancestor’s Tomb. Amidst a chaotic battle involving the Remnant of the Sword Sage{{!}}Sword Sage's Remnant and various guards, [[Lindon]] refuses an alliance with [[Whitehall]]. Using the [[Heart of Twin Stars]] technique to split his core, [[Lindon]] survives a lethal attack and escapes with [[Yerin]]. As they flee [[Sacred Valley]], [[Lindon]] realizes he is not truly unsouled, but is instead forging his own unique [[Path of Twin Stars]], while the [[Abidan Judges]] [[Suriel]] and [[Gadrael]] watch from the higher realms as worlds collide.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|In the [[Sacred Valley]], children undergo a [[Spiritual Origin test]] using a bowl of [[madra]] to determine their soul's nature and receive a corresponding wooden badge. Seven-year-old [[Lindon]] fails to elicit any reaction from the liquid, leading the [[First Elder]] to declare him "[[Unsouled]]" and deny him a badges{{!}}badge. Despite multiple attempts to sabotage the test with blood and runes to earn a status, [[Lindon]] is ultimately given a unique wooden badge marked only with the symbol for "empty."}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|While searching for an ancestral tree to obtain a spirit-fruit, [[Lindon]] successfully identifies one but is interrupted by his cousin Wei Mon Teris{{!}}Teris, who accidentally destroys the tree. The destruction releases a powerful Remnant{{!}}tree-Remnant that attacks [[Lindon]], but he manages to trap the spirit in a Script{{!}}script and successfully retrieves both the physical fruit and its [[Remnant]] counterpart. After returning home, [[Lindon]]'s family debates how to divide the potent fruit among themselves, leading him to ask for a share.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After a heated family argument, [[Lindon]] successfully claims half of the spirit-fruit he hunted, though its consumption yields no immediate advancement in his [[sacred arts]]. He is subsequently summoned by the [[First Elder]], who punishes his cousin Wei Mon Teris{{!}}Teris for cowardice and reprimands [[Lindon]] for the danger his presence caused the clan. To seek guidance, [[Lindon]] climbs a massive tower to feed [[Elder Whisper]], where the ancient sacred fox immediately senses the lingering energy of the fruit within him.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After seeking guidance from [[Elder Whisper]], [[Lindon]] receives cryptic encouragement to forge his own path despite being [[Unsouled]]. This resolve is tested when the [[Mon family]], seeking to deflect from their own shame, challenges [[Lindon]] to a duel of honor against their ten-year-old daughter, [[Eri]]. To prepare for the fight, [[Lindon]] searches the clan archives for shortcuts to advancement and eventually discovers a mysterious technique titled "[[Heart of Twin Stars]]."}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After discovering a manual for the "[[Empty Palm]]" technique, [[Lindon]] practices with his sister [[Kelsa]] to develop a way to disrupt an opponent's core. During the scheduled duel, [[Lindon]] uses his new technique and a desperate follow-up punch to successfully stagger the [[Iron]]-level [[Wei Mon Keth]], forcing a surrender that saves his family's honor. The confrontation ends when the [[First Elder]] uses an incredibly sophisticated illusion to subdue a furious Wei Mon Keth{{!}}Keth and resolve the conflict.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After a discussion with his father about the unique advantages of his [[pure madra]], [[Lindon]] negotiates with the [[First Elder]] to gain access to a [[Path]] if he can win an exhibition match at the upcoming [[Festival]]. Meanwhile, the [[Abidan]] [[Suriel]] arrives on the planet [[Cradle]] to investigate a fated spatial violation, choosing to intervene in a local crime to find a moment of peace away from her cosmic duties.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After intense training, [[Lindon]] witnesses his sister [[Kelsa]] successfully advance to the [[Iron stage]], an event celebrated by the [[Wei clan]]. Seeking an advantage for the upcoming [[Seven-Year Festival]], [[Lindon]] ventures into the woods to strike a deal with a swarm of hornet Remnants, trading a spirit crystal for their future assistance. He successfully captures the [[Remnants]] in a jar and attempts to hide them beneath the arena stage, only to be interrupted by his mother.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Lindon]] is confronted by his mother, [[Seisha]], while hiding a sealed jar in a hole, but she advises him to cover it up and expresses pride in him. [[Seisha]] reveals that the [[Li clan]] is suspiciously hunting specific [[Remnants]] in a potential, albeit unlikely, attempt to master spatial madra. The chapter concludes with an overview of the [[Seven-Year Festival]], a competitive event where the Wei, [[Kazan]], and [[Li]] clans display their power and cultural differences through elaborate arrivals and illusions.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|During the [[Seven-Year Festival]], [[Lindon]] uses a secret technique to easily win his [[Foundation]]-level fights, much to the confusion and jeering of the crowd. Meanwhile, [[Seisha]] discovers a group of [[Li clan]] elders in the forest performing a ritual to summon an ancient, winged [[Grand Patriarch]]. The summoned immortal begins marching toward the arena to assert his power, leaving [[Seisha]] trapped and fearing for her life.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After winning the [[Foundation stage]] tournament, [[Lindon]] defies [[Patriarch Sairus]] to face a senior disciple in an exhibition match. The event is violently interrupted by [[Li Markuth]], a [[Gold-level]] [[Grand Patriarch]] who arrives to settle an old clan debt by brutally murdering Sairus and displaying the severed heads of [[Lindon]]'s family. In a desperate attempt to fight back, [[Lindon]] strikes the intruder with an [[Empty Palm]] but is easily swatted aside, ultimately dying as he watches the [[Jade]] elders engage in a hopeless battle.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After a spatial trespasser kills [[Lindon]] during a festival, the celestial being [[Suriel]] intervenes to reverse causality, restoring [[Lindon]]'s life and repairing the damage. She shows [[Lindon]] visions of his potential [[fate]]—a prosperous life that ends in the destruction of his home—and introduces him to powerful figures across the world to inspire his growth. Ultimately, [[Suriel]] grants [[Lindon]] her memories of these events and a celestial token, instructing him to leave [[Sacred Valley]] and pursue the [[sacred arts]] so they may meet again.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After using a swarm of [[Remnants]] to defeat his cousin [[Wei Jin Amon]], [[Lindon]] earns a conditional place as a disciple of the Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School. Despite his family's concern and his own internal turmoil regarding a celestial vision, [[Lindon]] departs [[Sacred Valley]] to join the school. Upon arriving at [[Mount Samara]], he faces the [[Trial of Glorious Ascension]], a dangerous climb through spirit-aspect [[Remnants]] that serves as the first test for new disciples.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|To avoid suspicion, [[Lindon]] disguises himself as a wounded herb gatherer to secure a ride in a [[Heaven's Glory]] carriage after reaching the top of the mountain. Upon arrival, he encounters [[Elder Whitehall]], who is secretly planning to loot the treasures of a fallen [[Sword Sage]]. [[Lindon]] successfully deceives [[Whitehall]] by feigning submissive gratitude, allowing him to receive a token for the [[Lesser Treasure Hall]] and continue his mission.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After successfully navigating the [[Trial of Glorious Ascension]], [[Lindon]] arrives at the Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School and meets [[Elder Rahm]] at the [[Lesser Treasure Hall]]. While browsing various priceless artifacts, [[Lindon]] ultimately chooses a set of [[White Fox boundary formation]] banners to aid his training and honor his heritage. As he prepares to leave, he is confronted by an angry [[Iron]]-level disciple from the [[Kazan clan]].}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After using a Boundary formations{{!}}boundary formation to trick and incapacitate the [[Kazan]] disciple Ma Deret in a duel, [[Lindon]] receives valuable spirit-fruit and elixirs from [[Elder Anses]]. However, Deret soon ambushes [[Lindon]], brutally beating him and stealing his resources. The chapter concludes with a wounded [[Lindon]] being taken to the [[Medicine Hall]], where he overhears disciples discussing a mysterious powerful woman who recently attacked them.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After recovering from his injuries with a medicinal pill, [[Lindon]] infiltrates the mountain's north side to find [[Yerin]], the Sword Sage's Disciple{{!}}Sword Sage's disciple. He eventually locates her in a chasm and convinces her to form an alliance by swearing a mutual oath on their souls. The chapter concludes with [[Yerin]] warning [[Lindon]] that seven armed fighters are currently heading toward their location.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After [[Yerin]] fights off [[Elder Whitehall]] and his disciples, [[Lindon]] uses a Boundary formations{{!}}boundary formation to distract [[Kazan Ma Deret]], eventually killing him with a spear to ensure their survival. The two retreat to [[Lindon]]'s room at the Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School to rest, where [[Yerin]] reveals her intention to stay and harvest her master's [[Remnant]]. Realizing they are both trapped by their circumstances, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] form a tentative alliance to steal equipment from the school's [[Lesser Treasure Hall]].}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Elder Whitehall]], driven by greed for the [[Sword Sage]]'s treasures, arrives at the [[Ancestor's Tomb]] and feigns injury to gain shelter from [[Jade]] guards. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] infiltrate the [[Lesser Treasure Hall]] to steal sacred artifacts, leading to a confrontation with [[Elder Rahm]] and his constructs. [[Yerin]] uses a powerful [[sword]] resonance technique to incapacitate the elder, allowing them to escape on a stolen [[Thousand-Mile Cloud]] toward the [[Tomb]].}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] arrive at the Ancestor's Tomb{{!}}Ancestor’s Tomb, where they are immediately confronted by a powerful [[Remnant of the Sword Sage]] and guards from Heaven's Glory{{!}}Heaven’s Glory. During the chaotic battle, [[Lindon]] uses a halfsilver{{!}}halfsilver dagger to ambush [[Elder Whitehall]], who attempts to intervene and claim the treasures for himself. After [[Yerin]] successfully uses Spirit-seals{{!}}spirit-seals to weaken the [[Remnant]], [[Lindon]] retreats into the tomb and is confronted by a suspicious [[Whitehall]], who offers an alliance.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|After refusing [[Elder Whitehall]]'s offer to become his disciple, [[Lindon]] uses the [[Heart of Twin Stars]] technique to split his core, allowing him to survive [[Whitehall]]'s attack and escape the [[Ancestor's Tomb]] with [[Yerin]]. As they flee [[Sacred Valley]], [[Lindon]] realizes he is not truly unsouled but is instead forging his own unique [[Path of Twin Stars]]. Meanwhile, in the higher realms, the [[Abidan Judges]] [[Suriel]] and [[Gadrael]] observe the chaos of colliding worlds as they prepare to search for the missing Ozriel.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Suriel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Seisha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kelsa]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Elder Whisper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sword Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Wei Mon Teris]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[First Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Li Markuth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Wei Jin Amon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Wei Jin Sairus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kazan Ma Deret]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Jaran]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Gadrael]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Whitehall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Wei Mon Keth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Makiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|celestial messenger}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Elder Whitehall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Ozriel (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Lindon's mother]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Wei Shi Jaran]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Presence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|hornet Remnants}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Luminous Queen Sha Miara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sword Disciple]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnant of the Sword Sage]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sacred Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Wei clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Heaven’s Glory School]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Ancestor's Tomb]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Lesser Treasure Hall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Mount Samara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Li clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Cradle]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Mount Venture]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Heaven's Glory]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Fallen Leaf School]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kazan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Fallen Leaf]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Kazan clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sanctum]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[arena (Cradle)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Empty Palm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Heart of Twin Stars]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Foxfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Fox Mirror technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[White Fox]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Sword-aspect madra]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[parasite ring]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|orus fruit}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Greatfather’s tears]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Bloodmaker Pill]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[halfsilver]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Spirit-seals]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Suriel's token]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Starlotus Bud]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Path of the White Fox]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Unsouled]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Seven-Year Festival]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Jade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Trial of Glorious Ascension]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Gold]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Enforcer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Iron stage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Copper]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Striker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Ruler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Forger]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Copper stage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Samara's ring]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|soul-oath}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL20781727W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
